bar.name: Gold Room
bar.address: 1558 W Sunset Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90026
bar.happyHour: Daily specials, PBR + tequila shot combos
bar.vibe: Latino-owned dive bar, LGBTQ+ friendly, dark wood, jukebox, free peanuts, cash only
barFacts: 2009 upscale remodel with $15 champagne alienated regulars — minor gentrification controversy
Immortalized in Dave Lefner's color linocut print series

stops.label: Echo Park Lake & Lady of the Lake

pois.name: Angelus Temple
facts: 5,300-seat megachurch — largest in America when built
Founded by Aimee Semple McPherson, first woman to preach on radio
Dome coated with ground abalone shells, giving it a distinctive shimmer

McPherson vanished from Venice Beach May 18, 1926 — two people died searching. Reappeared 5 weeks later claiming kidnapping. Witnesses placed her in Carmel with married radio engineer Kenneth Ormiston
Pioneered 'illustrated sermons' — theatrical productions with sets, costumes, lighting
Founded International Church of the Foursquare Gospel (8M+ members, 146 countries)
Cornerstone inscription: 'Dedicated unto the cause of inter-denominational and worldwide evangelism'
Architect: Brook Hawkins. Class A fireproof construction
8 stained glass windows by George Haskins depicting scenes from Jesus's life
McPherson imported lotus flowers from China, planted them in Echo Park Lake in the 1920s
After founding, Echo Park's population surged as followers moved closer to 'Sister Aimee'
Bronze NPS plaque reported missing as of 2024

features.detail: Massive unsupported concrete dome — one of largest in North America when built
features.material: concrete coated with ground abalone shells

features.detail: Lighted cross atop dome — visible LA landmark
Semicircular facade described as 'half Roman Colosseum, half Parisian opera house'

features.text: Dedicated unto the cause of inter-denominational and worldwide evangelism

features.detail: 8 stained glass windows by George Haskins
Height approximately 28 meters (from Overpass data)

features.text: National Historic Landmark plaque — reportedly missing as of 2024

connections.link: McPherson imported lotus from China, planted in the lake in the 1920s
Population surged after founding as followers moved to be near Sister Aimee
Built within a year of each other during Echo Park's 1920s boom

pois.name: Echo Park Lake
facts: Started as a reservoir in 1868 to power a woolen mill at 6th and Figueroa
Named 'Echo Park' by parks superintendent Joseph Henry Tomlinson in 1892, who heard echoes shouting across the arroyo during construction
Lotus flowers planted by Aimee Semple McPherson's missionaries in the 1920s with seeds from China
In 2005, a horticulturalist illegally pilfered a lotus cutting, cultivated 376 plants, and sold them for $30,000
Keystone Studios (Keystone Cops) were banned from filming here because they trampled too many flowers
At least 60 people have drowned in the lake over its 100+ year history
Boathouse built 1932 with Depression-era unemployment bonds
Pedal boats have circled the lake since 1932
Annual Lotus Festival since 1972 ('Day of the Lotus') celebrates Asian and Pacific Islander communities

features.detail: Boathouse structure at east end of lake
Lotus beds — seasonal, bloom July/August
Pedal boats (swan-shaped) on the water
Fountain visible in the lake

connections.link: Planted lotus from China in the lake
Named the park for the echoes he heard
Banned from filming for trampling flowers
Statue on the lake's peninsula since 1935

pois.name: Lady of the Lake / Nuestra Reina de Los Angeles
facts: 14-foot Art Deco cast stone statue by Ada Mae Sharpless (born Hilo, Hawaii 1904, studied in Paris)
Official name: 'Nuestra Reina de Los Angeles' (Queen of the Angels)
WPA/PWAP commission, 1934-35. Originally planned in bronze — Depression-era budget cuts forced cast stone ('liquid stone' / poured concrete)
Pedestal has 4 bas-relief panels depicting: Hollywood Bowl, LA Harbor, San Gabriel Mountains, LA Central Library
Vandalized, covered in graffiti, fingers broken off. Put in storage 1986. Restored and returned 1999 with anti-graffiti sealant
Conserved by City of LA Cultural Affairs with Councilmember Jackie Goldberg, DWP, Rec & Parks, Echo Park Historical Society

features.detail: 14-foot Art Deco female figure on pedestal
Panel 1: Hollywood Bowl
Panel 2: LA Harbor
Panel 3: San Gabriel Mountains
Panel 4: LA Central Library
Rose bushes surrounding the statue

connections.link: Both Depression-era projects — WPA statue, unemployment bond boathouse
Both monumental structures built by women (Sharpless / McPherson) within 0.3mi of each other

pois.name: Jensen's Recreation Center
facts: Built by Henry Christian Jensen, German immigrant who made fortune manufacturing bricks for LA's expansion
Architect: E.E.B. Meinardus. Three stories: bowling/pool at street level, 46 apartments with Murphy beds above
Rooftop sign: 28 ft wide × 17 ft tall, 1,300 red/green/white incandescent bulbs
Sign depicts animated bowler throwing strike — motors switch bulbs on/off to simulate motion
Billed as the last remaining articulated incandescent sign in the United States
Sign dark for ~50 years, restored by Paul Greenstein. Building sold for $15M in 2014
Egyptian-inspired ornamentation wraps around walls near ceiling of large interior rooms
'Jensen Recreation Center' spelled in large terra cotta letters above front entrance
Corner of Sunset Blvd & Logan Street

features.detail: Three-story brick building
Some original decorative tilework covered by modern storefront signage

connections.link: Built within a year — Echo Park's 1920s building boom
Jensen's bricks literally built the city; then he built himself a building
Same block of Sunset Blvd, 0.06mi apart

pois.name: Avenue of the Athletes
pois.address: Sunset Blvd sidewalks, McDuff St to Mohawk St
facts: 32 brass sidewalk plaques (17×16 inches each) honoring athletes, many Olympians with LA connections
Created by L. Andrew Castle — local business owner, Dodgers photographer, and professor
Castle conceived the project, got city approval in 1974. First plaques placed 1976, second set 1977
Castle died in 1978, just 2 years after the first plaques were placed. Project stopped.
Plaque designs were literally lost for years before Chamber of Commerce revived the project in 1980
Honorees include: Jesse Owens, Jackie Robinson, Sandy Koufax, Billie Jean King, Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, Elgin Baylor, Wyomia Tyus, Kenny Washington
10 blocks of Sunset Blvd between Elysian Park Blvd and Alvarado St declared 'Avenue of the Athletes'
Most people walk over the plaques every day without noticing them

features.text: Bronze sidewalk plaques with athlete names and sport pictograms

features.detail: Plaques blend into gray concrete — easy to miss, you have to look down

connections.link: Plaques are IN the sidewalk at the Gold Room's block
Castle wanted to bring tourists to Echo Park — the walk of fame nobody notices

pois.name: 826LA / Echo Park Time Travel Mart
facts: Storefront themed as 'a convenience store for time travelers'
Motto: 'Whenever You Are, We're Already There'
Products include: Viking Odorant, Robot Milk, Robot Toupees, Mammoth Chunks, Robot Emotions, Time-Freezy Hyper Slush
Behind a secret door in the back: free tutoring center where 826LA teaches 20-30 students per day
All proceeds fund 826LA nonprofit for students ages 6-18
Part of 826 National network co-founded by Dave Eggers (SF's Pirate Supply Store was the first)
LA chose time travel as its theme. Student publications displayed in a freezer.
Window displays caveman shaking hands with robot

features.text: Whenever You Are, We're Already There

features.detail: Caveman shaking hands with robot in window
Time travel products visible through window — Robot Milk, Viking Odorant, etc.
Slushy machine with sign 'Out of Order Come Back Yesterday'
Poster with facial hair styles throughout history visible through window

connections.link: Both are things that aren't what they appear from outside

pois.name: Jose Marti Monument
pois.address: Glendale Blvd & Park Ave (near Echo Park Lake)
facts: Bronze bust by sculptor Sergio Lopez Mesa (born Havana, studied at San Alejandro Academy and School of Fine Arts in Rome)
Commemorates Jose Marti (born Havana 1853, died in battle at Dos Rios, Cuba, May 19, 1895, fighting for Cuban independence from Spain)
Built 1976 by Patronato Jose Marti with City of LA Recreation and Parks
Restored by LA Cultural Affairs Dept in 2002
Echo Park became the social/cultural heart of LA's Cuban immigrant community after 1962, when a local Presbyterian church met the first flight of Cuban refugees
Inscription: 'El que tenga Patria que la honre, el que no, que la conquiste' — 'He who has a homeland, honor it; he who does not, conquer one'

features.detail: Bronze bust on pedestal

features.text: El que tenga Patria que la honre, el que no, que la conquiste
Con todos y para el bien de todos

connections.link: Park became Cuban cultural heart after 1962 refugee flights
At the edge of the lake

pois.name: Taix French Restaurant
facts: French restaurant founded 1927 by Marius Taix Jr.
Moved to Echo Park location 1962, remodeled 1969
Features 321 Lounge (cocktail lounge with live music) and banquet halls
PERMANENTLY CLOSING MARCH 29, 2026 — after 99 years of operation
Sunset & Glendale Blvd Viaduct (nearby) built 1934, New Deal Federal Public Works program

features.detail: Large restaurant complex with multiple dining rooms

pois.name: Carroll Avenue Historic District
facts: Finest collection of Victorian domestic architecture remaining in Los Angeles
Queen Anne and Eastlake styles from the late 19th century
Angelino Heights subdivided 1886 by William Stilson and Everett Hall during land boom
Banking recession of 1888 halted construction — accidentally preserving the Victorian character
1329 Carroll Ave: The 'Charmed' house (Halliwell Manor exterior)
1345 Carroll Ave: Featured in Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' video
The Foy House: Oldest on the block, built 1872, moved twice

features.detail: Queen Anne facades with turrets, gingerbread trim, ornate porches
Eastlake style wooden detailing

pois.routeNote: Outside 0.5mi radius — supplemental POI, not a route stop

pois.name: Phantasma Gloria at Randyland
facts: 24-foot-tall, 50-foot-long sculpture in a residential front yard
Built by artist Randlett Lawrence entirely from glass bottles, wire, and colored water
Bottles refract morning sunlight into prismatic color — best seen around 9am on clear days
Patterns inside bottles include stylized dolphins and a Virgin de Guadalupe honoring Echo Park's multicultural community
Still a work in progress

features.detail: 24-ft tall glass bottle sculpture visible from street
In a residential front yard — looks like outsider art installation

pois.name: Tom of Finland House (TOM House)
facts: Studio and residence of Finnish queer artist Touko Laaksonen (Tom of Finland) from 1980-1991
House built 1911
World's largest repository of erotic art — over 100,000 images
Now the Tom of Finland Foundation, open by appointment (Thurs/Sat 2-4pm)
Declared HCM in 2016 by Cultural Heritage Commission

features.detail: Two-story residential house, built 1911

pois.name: Healthy Foods Mosaic
facts: Mosaic artwork by artist Katrina Alexy
Located on or near Sunset Blvd in Echo Park

features.detail: Wall mosaic

stop1_angelusTemple.notes: McPherson bio is a natural 3-hint narrative riddle. Dome + abalone coating is a reframe. Cornerstone text is a spotter. Lotus connection to lake is a connector.

stop2_echoParkLake.notes: Lady of the Lake's 4 bas-reliefs are countable and identifiable (spotter gold). 'What was this lake before it was a park?' is a reframe. Reservoir→park→lotus→festival is a sequence. Jose Marti inscription is a cipher-lite source.

stop3_jensensSunsetBlock.notes: Jensen's sign (1,300 bulbs, animated bowler) is the richest spotter in the system. Avenue of Athletes plaques are countable, nameable — elimination and sequence gold. 826LA's 'time travel store is actually a tutoring center' is a perfect reframe. Castle dying 2 years after starting the project is a narrative riddle.

overpassBuildingStock.note: Echo Park's building stock is overwhelmingly pre-war. 69% of dated buildings were constructed 1900-1929.
